principles: Models must have named versions, intended uses, limitations, provenance, and expiry or review expectations.
Forecasts remain distinguishable from user-authored scenarios and deterministic projections.
Evaluation metrics must be tied to overlapping forecast and observed periods.
Uncertainty intervals, calibration gaps, and drift indicators must remain visible when available.
Threshold crossings are review signals, not proof of cause or guaranteed outcomes.
boundaries: No autonomous legal, humanitarian, financial, medical, employment, credit, policing, military, or emergency decisions.
No individual-level targeting, surveillance, or personal risk scoring.
No guaranteed-outcome, emergency-service, or official-warning claims.
No hidden model substitution, silent retraining, or unlabeled forecast revision.
No conversion of scenarios into forecasts without an attributable registered model.
default_prohibited_uses: Guaranteed financial, legal, medical, or humanitarian outcomes
